title: thymus serpyllum subsp. arcticus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. arcticus, also known as Arctic Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, flowering perennial native to high-altitude regions in Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Arctic Thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Arctic Thyme can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
